raw:
All wiring looks correct. Let me check the test file.
Test is well-structured. Let me run it to confirm it passes.
Test passes and is executable. Let me verify it exits non-zero on failure.
Everything checks out. The implementation fully satisfies the task:

- `diff.ts` created with the exact specified content; imports only from `memory.ts` (node-free).
- `learn.ts`: captures `beforeMarkdown` after `ensureLearnedScaffold`, re-reads after clean exit, computes `diff` via `diffLearnedMarkdown`, all best-effort in try/catch; existing fields (`deltaCount`, `updaterText`, `reason`, index refresh) intact.
- `index.ts`: replaces the plain success `notify()` with colored `renderDiffLines` via `theme.fg` (toolDiffAdded/toolDiffRemoved/dim/accent), shows it with `setWidget("continual-learning-diff", lines, {placement:"aboveEditor"})`, auto-dismisses on a 30s timer (reset on new pass) AND on `agent_start`; falls back to `notify()` when diff empty/unavailable; same widget path applied to the manual command; all `ctx.ui` calls guarded with optional chaining; warning/failed paths kept as `notify("warning")`.
- `test/diff_test.sh`: executable, modeled on `learn_test.sh`, covers add/remove/reword-as-changed, `isEmptyDiff` true on identical, and `renderDiffLines` title counts + maxLines budget with overflow footer; prints "diff_test: ALL PASS"; exits non-zero on failure (set -e + assert).

Test runs green; gates aggregate exit code 0.

VERDICT: PASS
